steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function can be written as $f(x)=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the value of $f(0)$.

steps.title: Substitute the graph details
steps.content:
The slope is $54$, so $m=54$. The graph passes through $(0,0)$, so the y-intercept is $0$.
$$f(x)=54x+0=54x$$

steps.title: Match the equation
steps.content: The equation with slope $54$ and y-intercept $0$ is $f(x)=54x$. The answer is A.
